forward_pass.phase_summary: The group discussed the need to scale outreach, matchmaking, training, and onboarding activities to generate sufficient problem statements and pairings for the 100E programme, given S$500 million funding, 50 trainers, and 500,000 GPU hours. There was strong consensus on the causal link between these activities and achieving the target of 100 projects, with most participants converging on estimates of 110 enterprise outreach sessions, 28 matchmaking events, 38 training workshops, and 190 platform onboarding assists. Minor disagreements centered on the precise scale of outreach, with one participant proposing 120 sessions and another 100, reflecting differing views on enterprise caution and coordination constraints. The overall numeric range was narrow: enterprise outreach sessions 100–120, matchmaking events 25–30, training workshops 35–40, and onboarding assists 180–200, with central tendencies near 110, 28, 38, and 190 respectively.

initial_posts.narrative: Given the inputs of S$500 million, co-funding cap of S$330,000 per project, 500,000 GPU hours, and 50 trainers, and the activities of 100 outreach sessions, 25 matchmaking events, 35 training workshops, and 180 onboarding assists, I estimate that the 100E programme will approve 100 projects in 2023, matching the programme's name and target. However, not all approved projects will result in a fully built prototype due to technical challenges and enterprise readiness; I estimate 85 prototypes will be developed. The AIAP and other training programmes, with 35 workshops and 50 trainers, can produce about 200 trainees completing the programme (assuming ~6 trainees per workshop on average, accounting for dropouts). Finally, about 80 enterprises will have at least one AI solution, as some enterprises may participate in multiple projects or fail to deploy a solution. My estimates are slightly conservative compared to the maximum possible, reflecting practical constraints like data privacy concerns and the need for careful validation.

Enterprises often need multiple iterations or may abandon projects; I estimate 80% of enterprises that started a project will end up with a deployable AI solution, resulting in 80 enterprises with a solution.

initial_posts.narrative: As a digital marketing specialist on my company's AI task force, I see the 100E programme aiming for 100 projects, and with S$500 million funding and co-funding up to S$330K per project, I believe the target will be met exactly in 2023. However, not all approved projects will yield a working prototype due to technical hurdles and enterprise readiness issues; I estimate 80 prototypes will be built, slightly lower than Dr. (na5ra9)'s 85 because I've seen firsthand how retail enterprises struggle with data quality and integration. forward_pass.phase_summary: The group reached strong consensus on all four impact variables, with every participant predicting a global AI readiness rank of 5, AI sector GDP share of 3.5%, public AI trust score of 65%, and AI talent retention rate of 70%. Key causal mechanisms included the visible success of 100E projects (60 deployed) and 180 trained practitioners boosting Singapore's readiness rank, while cautious adoption in regulated sectors (legal, finance, healthcare) due to data privacy and integration challenges limited GDP growth and trust. Public trust was seen as constrained by persistent concerns over data privacy and job displacement despite responsible AI efforts, and talent retention was moderated by competitive global demand. No disagreements emerged in numeric estimates or reasoning, though some participants emphasized sector-specific barriers like privacy in healthcare or integration in retail.
